questions: You mention that the Responsible AI Checker only passed 12 of 25 checks (48% coverage) — what surprised you most about which categories had the biggest gaps, and did that change how you prioritized what to build next?

When Claude Code proactively added the `mode` parameter to CostGuard that you hadn't originally designed for, did that shift how you think about collaborating with AI on technical design, or was this just a helpful catch?

You drew a distinction between 'extrapolation' (acceptable) and 'invention' (problematic) in the grounding checker — how did you decide where that line should be, and did any of your test cases make you want to move it?
